text.title: JPA / Hibernate Anti-Patterns
text.summary: @Data on entities, EAGER fetch, CascadeType.ALL on @ManyToOne, skipped migration, OSIV enabled.

antipattern.description: Lombok @Data on JPA entities: generates equals/hashCode over all fields including lazy collections, causing infinite recursion and broken Set semantics.
antipattern.reason: @Data iterates all fields for equals; lazy collection fields trigger a DB load and form a cycle. Use @Getter @Setter only and implement equals/hashCode manually on a business key.

antipattern.description: FetchType.EAGER on @OneToMany or @ManyToMany to "be safe": every query loads all associated records, creating Cartesian explosions when multiple collections are fetched.
antipattern.reason: EAGER fetch cannot be selectively disabled at the query level; once declared on the mapping it applies to every find. It multiplies rows when joined with other EAGER collections, hitting MultipleBagFetchException.

antipattern.description: cascade = CascadeType.ALL on a @ManyToOne relationship: deleting the child cascades to the parent, wiping the parent entity and all its other children.
antipattern.reason: CascadeType.ALL on @ManyToOne propagates persist/merge/remove up the ownership chain — semantically backwards. Cascade is appropriate on @OneToMany from parent to children, not from child to parent.

antipattern.description: Changing an entity column type or renaming a column without a paired Flyway migration.
antipattern.reason: Hibernate schema validation (spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=validate) will throw a SchemaManagementException on startup. Production deployments without migrations cause downtime or data loss on column type changes.

antipattern.description: @Modifying on a bulk UPDATE without clearAutomatically = true: subsequent findById returns stale entity from first-level cache.
antipattern.reason: Bulk UPDATE bypasses Hibernate's entity lifecycle; the first-level cache (L1) is not aware of the change and returns the pre-update state. clearAutomatically flushes the L1 cache after the bulk operation.

antipattern.description: Using @SpringBootTest for repository slice tests instead of @DataJpaTest.
antipattern.reason: @SpringBootTest loads the full application context including web layer, security, and services. @DataJpaTest slices to just the JPA layer, boots 10x faster, and isolates schema issues from application logic.

antipattern.description: LazyInitializationException in controllers: entity with a lazy association is serialized outside the Hibernate session after the transaction closes.
antipattern.reason: OSIV (open-in-view) masks this problem in development by keeping the session open. With OSIV disabled (the correct setting), agents must use DTO projections or JOIN FETCH to load required associations within the transaction.
